sections.title: Parties and Relationship Status
sections.purpose: Identifies the parties, their legal names, addresses, date of birth, and whether they qualify as 'spouses' under marriage or cohabitation (FLA s. 3), which determines applicable property and support regimes.

sections.title: Recitals and Background
sections.purpose: Sets out the factual matrix: date of marriage or cohabitation, date of separation, intention to settle all matters, and any interim arrangements already in place.

sections.title: Definitions
sections.purpose: Defines key terms used throughout the agreement, including 'family property', 'excluded property', 'family debt', 'separation date', and 'triggering event', aligned with FLA statutory definitions.

sections.title: Validity and Procedural-Fairness Preconditions
sections.purpose: Captures the procedural-fairness factors courts test when assessing enforceability: voluntariness, absence of duress or undue influence, independent legal advice, full financial disclosure, and understanding of consequences, per FLA s. 93 and the Rick v. Brandsema, Miglin v. Miglin, and Hartshorne v. Hartshorne frameworks.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA s. 93; Rick v. Brandsema 2009 SCC 10; Miglin v. Miglin 2003 SCC 24; Hartshorne v. Hartshorne 2004 SCC 22

sections.title: Financial Disclosure Attestations
sections.purpose: Records each party's sworn or affirmed attestation of full, frank, and complete disclosure of income, assets, liabilities, and other financial information, equivalent to Form F8 financial statements under the Supreme Court Family Rules.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA s. 93(3)(a); Supreme Court Family Rules, Rule 5-1 / Form F8

sections.title: Children of the Relationship
sections.purpose: Identifies all children of the relationship, including birth dates, parentage, and any children to whom either party stands in the place of a parent (FLA s. 147).

sections.title: Parenting Arrangements
sections.purpose: Sets out parenting time allocation, parental responsibilities (decision-making authority), communication protocols between parents, relocation provisions, and travel/passport consent, all assessed against the best interests of the child under FLA s. 37.

sections.title: Child Support
sections.purpose: Establishes child support obligations, including table amounts under the Federal Child Support Guidelines, special and extraordinary expenses (s. 7 expenses), duration, income disclosure obligations, and annual review mechanisms.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA Part 7 (ss. 147-154 [VERIFY]); Federal Child Support Guidelines, SOR/97-175; Divorce Act s. 15.1 (if applicable)

sections.title: Spousal Support
sections.purpose: Addresses entitlement, quantum, duration, and form (periodic or lump sum) of spousal support, with reference to compensatory and non-compensatory bases, the Spousal Support Advisory Guidelines, and review/variation triggers.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA Part 7 (ss. 160-172 [VERIFY]); Divorce Act ss. 15.2-17 (if applicable); Moge v. Moge [1992] 3 SCR 813; Bracklow v. Bracklow [1999] 1 SCR 420

sections.title: Property Division (Family and Excluded Property)
sections.purpose: Divides family property equally (or sets out an agreed unequal division with reasons) and identifies excluded property under FLA s. 85, including traced excluded property, gifts, inheritances, and pre-relationship property.

sections.title: Real Property Specifics
sections.purpose: Addresses the family home and any other real property, including transfer, sale, exclusive possession, timing of listing, mortgage discharge obligations, and Land Title Office registration requirements.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA Part 5; Land Title Act [RSBC 1996] c. 250

sections.title: Family Debt Allocation
sections.purpose: Allocates responsibility for family debts as defined in FLA s. 86, including mortgages, lines of credit, credit cards, and tax liabilities, and includes indemnification provisions.

sections.title: Pension Division
sections.purpose: Addresses division of pension entitlements, including BC public sector pensions divisible under the Pension Benefits Standards Act and the FLA pension division framework, specifying the proportionate share or lump-sum transfer mechanism.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA Part 6 (ss. 113-127 [VERIFY]); Pension Benefits Standards Act, S.B.C. 2012, c. 30 [VERIFY]

sections.title: Life Insurance and Security
sections.purpose: Requires parties to maintain life insurance policies or other security to secure ongoing support obligations and specifies beneficiary designations, minimum coverage amounts, and proof-of-insurance protocols.

sections.title: Tax Treatment and Indemnities
sections.purpose: Allocates the tax consequences of property transfers, support payments, RRSP withdrawals, and capital gains, and includes mutual tax indemnification obligations.
sections.fla_anchor: common law / contract; Income Tax Act (Canada), R.S.C. 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.), ss. 56, 60, 73

sections.title: Mutual Releases and Waivers
sections.purpose: Contains mutual general releases of all claims arising from the relationship except those preserved by the agreement, including wills variation claims under WESA s. 60 and any other statutory or equitable claims.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA s. 93; Wills, Estates and Succession Act, S.B.C. 2009, c. 13, s. 60

sections.title: Dispute Resolution
sections.purpose: Establishes a graduated dispute resolution process (negotiation, mediation, arbitration, or court) for disagreements arising from interpretation or breach of the agreement, consistent with FLA Part 2 encouragement of non-court resolution.

sections.title: Governing Law and Jurisdiction
sections.purpose: Specifies that the agreement is governed by British Columbia law, confirms the parties' attornment to BC courts, and addresses which court (Supreme Court of BC) has jurisdiction to enforce or vary the agreement.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA s. 1 (application); Court Jurisdiction and Proceedings Transfer Act, S.B.C. 2003, c. 28 [VERIFY]

sections.title: Conditions Precedent
sections.purpose: Lists any conditions that must be satisfied before the agreement or specific provisions come into force, such as granting of a divorce order, completion of a property transfer, or receipt of a pension valuation.

sections.title: General Provisions (Boilerplate)
sections.purpose: Includes severability, entire agreement, amendment requirements (written and signed), waiver provisions, further assurances, enurement, and provisions surviving separation or divorce.

sections.title: Execution, Witnessing, and ILA Certificates
sections.purpose: Contains the signature blocks for both parties and witnesses, certificates of independent legal advice for each party (a key factor under FLA s. 93 and Hartshorne), and a certificate of execution to satisfy FLA s. 92 requirements for written, signed family law agreements.
sections.fla_anchor: FLA s. 92, s. 93; Hartshorne v. Hartshorne 2004 SCC 22

sections.title: Schedules and Appendices
sections.purpose: Houses all attachments incorporated by reference, including the parenting schedule, property and debt inventory, pension valuations, financial disclosure summaries, and any parenting coordination protocol.
